# Ashen Pantheon Wiki Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Scaffold a full MkDocs Material wiki for Ashen Pantheon and deploy it to GitHub Pages at `https://katassyn.github.io/ashen-pantheon/`.

**Architecture:** Static site generated by MkDocs Material from Markdown files in `docs/`. GitHub Actions CI automatically builds and deploys on every push to `main` by running `mkdocs gh-deploy --force`, which pushes the built site to the `gh-pages` branch.

**Tech Stack:** MkDocs Material ≥9.5, mkdocs-git-revision-date-localized-plugin, mkdocs-minify-plugin, Python 3.11, GitHub Actions, GitHub Pages.

---

## File Map

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `mkdocs.yml` | Full site config: theme, nav, plugins |
| `requirements.txt` | Pinned Python deps for local + CI |

| `docs/index.md` | Home page with cards and at-a-glance table |

## Task 1: Python Environment + `requirements.txt` + `mkdocs.yml`

**Files:**
- Create: `requirements.txt`
- Create: `mkdocs.yml`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create `requirements.txt`**

```
mkdocs-material>=9.5
mkdocs-git-revision-date-localized-plugin>=1.2
mkdocs-minify-plugin>=0.8
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Install dependencies**

Run from the project root (`C:\Users\mastu\Desktop\Ashen Pantheon`):

```bash
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

Expected: all three packages install without errors.

- [ ] **Step 3: Create `mkdocs.yml`**

```yaml
site_name: Ashen Pantheon Wiki
site_url: https://katassyn.github.io/ashen-pantheon/
repo_url: https://github.com/katassyn/ashen-pantheon
repo_name: katassyn/ashen-pantheon

theme:
  name: material
  logo: logo_256x256.png
  favicon: logo_256x256.png
  palette:
    scheme: slate
    primary: amber
    accent: amber
  features:
    - navigation.tabs
    - navigation.sections
    - navigation.top
    - search.suggest
    - search.highlight

plugins:
  - search
  - tags
  - git-revision-date-localized:
      enable_creation_date: true
  - minify:
      minify_html: true

nav:
  - Home: index.md
  - Game Overview: game-overview.md
  - Classes:
    - Overview: classes/index.md
    - Warrior: classes/warrior.md
    - Mage: classes/mage.md
    - Archer: classes/archer.md
  - Ascendancies:
    - Berserker: ascendancies/berserker.md
    - Guardian: ascendancies/guardian.md
    - Paladin: ascendancies/paladin.md
    - Elementalist: ascendancies/elementalist.md
    - Arcanist: ascendancies/arcanist.md
    - Hexblade: ascendancies/hexblade.md
    - Shadowstalker: ascendancies/shadowstalker.md
    - Beastmaster: ascendancies/beastmaster.md
    - Pathfinder: ascendancies/pathfinder.md
  - Systems:
    - Progression: systems/progression.md
    - Skill Tree: systems/skill-tree.md
    - Ascendancy Tree: systems/ascendancy-tree.md
    - Skill Slots: systems/skill-slots.md
    - Instancing: systems/instancing.md
  - Bosses:
    - Overview: bosses/index.md
    - Boss Phases: bosses/boss-phases.md
  - World:
    - Overview: world/index.md
    - Maps: world/maps.md
  - Devlog:
    - Hub: devlog/index.md
    - "#001 — The Beginning": devlog/001-the-beginning.md
  - Tags: tags.md
```

---

## Task 2: GitHub Actions Deployment Workflow

**Files:**
- Create: `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create workflow directories**

```bash
mkdir -p .github/workflows
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Create `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`**

```yaml
name: Deploy Wiki to GitHub Pages

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

permissions:
  contents: write

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: pip install -r requirements.txt

      - name: Deploy
        run: mkdocs gh-deploy --force
```

Note: `fetch-depth: 0` is required so `git-revision-date-localized` can read commit history for "last updated" dates.

**Files:**

```markdown
---
tags: []
hide:
  - toc
---

# Ashen Pantheon Wiki

> *Forge your legend. Face the gods. Claim the Ashen Throne.*

**Ashen Pantheon** is a top-down 2D instanced action RPG where heroes battle mythological gods drawn from Greek, Norse, Egyptian, Japanese, and Slavic legend. Choose your class, master your ascendancy, and tear through multi-phase boss encounters on your path to level 100.

<div class="grid cards" markdown>

- :material-sword: **[Classes](classes/index.md)**

    Three base classes — Warrior, Mage, Archer — each branching into 3 ascendancy paths at level 50.

- :material-tree: **[Systems](systems/progression.md)**

    A two-phase skill tree system, free keybind assignment, and deep progression from level 1 to 100.

- :material-skull: **[Bosses](bosses/index.md)**

    Multi-phase mythological bosses drawn from real pantheons. No two fights play the same.

- :material-earth: **[World](world/index.md)**

    Player-instanced worlds with a shared global layer for chat, rankings, and trading.

- :material-newspaper: **[Devlog](devlog/index.md)**

    Follow the development of Ashen Pantheon from concept to release.

</div>

## At a Glance

| Feature | Detail |
|---|---|
| Genre | Top-down 2D Action RPG |
| Level Cap | 100 |
| Base Classes | 3 (Warrior, Mage, Archer) |
| Ascendancies | 9 — 3 per class, chosen at level 50 |
| Max Party Size | 4 |
| World Type | Player-owned instances |
| Boss Mythologies | Greek · Norse · Egyptian · Japanese · Slavic |
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Create `docs/game-overview.md`**

```markdown
---
tags: [overview, lore]
---

# Game Overview

Ashen Pantheon is set in a fractured realm where the boundaries between mythological pantheons have collapsed. Ancient gods — stripped of their divine order — have gone mad, their power seeping into a world of ash and ruin. You are an **Ashen Hunter**: a mortal who has absorbed enough divine essence to stand against them.

## Core Concept

The game blends responsive top-down action combat with the depth of an ARPG progression system. Every encounter is hand-crafted, every boss tells a story, and every build decision carries weight. The world is yours — but the gods want it back.

## The Instanced World

Each player owns their own **world instance** — a personal version of the game map that only they control. You explore, clear zones, and progress at your own pace without interference from other players.

When you want company, you can invite up to **3 friends** to join your instance, forming a party of 4.

### Global Layer

Even in an instanced world, you're never truly alone. A persistent **global layer** connects all players across instances:

| Layer Feature | Description |
|---|---|
| Chat | Cross-instance channels — general, trade, guild |
| Rankings | Leaderboards for level, boss kills, gear score |
| Trading | Player-to-player item exchange across instances |

## Progression at a Glance

| Phase | Levels | What Changes |
|---|---|---|
| Early Game | 1 – 49 | Main skill tree — broad, many nodes, class skills unlocked |
| Ascendancy | 50 | Choose one ascendancy path (permanent — choose wisely) |
| Mid/Late Game | 51 – 100 | Ascendancy tree — 1 point per 2 levels = 25 total points, fewer but far stronger nodes |
| Endgame | 100 | Gear farming, challenge bosses, rankings |

## The Bosses

Every major boss in Ashen Pantheon is drawn from real-world mythology. Each boss has a unique visual identity, lore connection, and a **multi-phase fight** that escalates in scope and intensity.

**Mythologies represented:**

- **Greek** — Olympian gods and ancient monsters
- **Norse** — Aesir, Vanir, and primordial beasts
- **Egyptian** — Ennead deities and pharaonic guardians
- **Japanese** — Kami and yokai of the spirit world
- **Slavic** — Ancient gods and forest spirits

[TODO: Add full boss roster as bosses are designed and named]
```

```markdown
---
tags: [classes]
---

# Classes

Ashen Pantheon has **three base classes**. Each class shapes your early playstyle and determines which ascendancies you can choose at level 50. The class you pick at character creation is permanent.

| Class | Role | Ascendancies |
|---|---|---|
| [Warrior](warrior.md) | Melee frontline, high HP, physical power | Berserker · Guardian · Paladin |
| [Mage](mage.md) | Ranged caster, elemental mastery, summons | Elementalist · Arcanist · Hexblade |
| [Archer](archer.md) | Ranged precision, mobility, pet synergies | Shadowstalker · Beastmaster · Pathfinder |

## Choosing a Class

Your base class affects:

- **Starting stats** — health, mana, stamina distribution
- **Skill tree shape** — each class has unique skill tree nodes alongside shared nodes
- **Available ascendancies** — you can only pick from your class's three ascendancy paths
- **Weapon proficiency** — Warriors favor melee weapons; Mages favor staves and orbs; Archers favor bows and daggers

[TODO: Add base stat tables per class once finalized]
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Create `docs/classes/warrior.md`**

```markdown
---
tags: [classes, warrior]
---

# Warrior

> *Where others fall back, the Warrior steps forward.*

The Warrior is the backbone of any party and a relentless solo force. Masters of close-quarters combat, Warriors absorb punishment, dish out crushing physical damage, and bend the flow of battle to their will. Whether you charge into the thick of it, hold the line while allies recover, or channel divine power into holy wrath — the Warrior path is defined by presence.

## Base Stats

| Stat | Warrior Start |
|---|---|
| Health | High |
| Mana | Low |
| Stamina | High |
| Physical Defense | High |
| Magic Defense | Low |

[TODO: Add exact base values once finalized]

## Playstyle

Warriors thrive in melee range. Your skill tree grants access to gap closers, crowd control, defensive stances, and raw physical damage multipliers. You are built to be where the fight is.

## Ascendancy Paths

At level 50 you choose one of three paths — this choice is **permanent**:

| Ascendancy | Summary |
|---|---|
| [Berserker](../ascendancies/berserker.md) | Rage-fueled damage dealer — lower HP means higher damage, sustained by lifesteal |
| [Guardian](../ascendancies/guardian.md) | Immovable tank — taunt, party shields, aggro management |
| [Paladin](../ascendancies/paladin.md) | Holy warrior — AoE divine damage, heals, and party-wide aura buffs |

## Skill Tree Highlights

[TODO: Add skill tree node highlights once tree is designed]

## Tips

- Warriors benefit enormously from gear with high physical defense and bonus health.
- Berserker builds want to stay below 50% HP — plan your lifesteal accordingly.
- Guardian's taunt mechanic is essential in 4-player parties — communicate with your team.
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Create `docs/classes/mage.md`**

```markdown
---
tags: [classes, mage]
---

# Mage

> *The elements are not forces to be controlled — they are arguments to be won.*

The Mage wields the raw stuff of the cosmos. Where the Warrior wins through strength and the Archer wins through precision, the Mage wins through understanding — reading the battlefield, stacking elemental reactions, and unleashing damage that no armor can fully stop. Mages are fragile but devastating.

## Base Stats

| Stat | Mage Start |
|---|---|
| Health | Low |
| Mana | High |
| Stamina | Medium |
| Physical Defense | Low |
| Magic Defense | High |

[TODO: Add exact base values once finalized]

## Playstyle

Mages operate from range, managing mana pools and elemental combinations. Your skill tree unlocks elemental affinities, cast speed modifiers, summon slots, and aura nodes. You reward deliberate, thoughtful play over button mashing.

## Ascendancy Paths

At level 50 you choose one of three paths — this choice is **permanent**:

| Ascendancy | Summary |
|---|---|
| [Elementalist](../ascendancies/elementalist.md) | Master of Fire, Ice, and Lightning — elemental mixing and massive AoE bursts |
| [Arcanist](../ascendancies/arcanist.md) | Elemental summoner — conjure entities, micromanage pets, hybrid support |
| [Hexblade](../ascendancies/hexblade.md) | Chaos mage — AoE auras, debilitating curses, damage-over-time |

## Skill Tree Highlights

[TODO: Add skill tree node highlights once tree is designed]

## Tips

- Mages have low health — invest in magic defense gear and consider movement skills for escape.
- Elementalist builds synergize around applying multiple elements to a single target.
- Hexblade curses stack multiplicatively with DoT — apply the curse first, then the DoT.
```

```markdown
---

# Archer

> *You never see the shot that kills you.*

The Archer is the most versatile base class — equally comfortable fighting from the treetops, vanishing into shadow, commanding a pack of mythological beasts, or laying a minefield of traps. Archers are mobile, resourceful, and often the most unpredictable player on the battlefield.

## Base Stats

| Stat | Archer Start |
|---|---|
| Health | Medium |
| Mana | Medium |
| Stamina | High |
| Physical Defense | Medium |
| Magic Defense | Medium |

[TODO: Add exact base values once finalized]

## Playstyle

Archers excel at managing distance, using terrain, and maintaining sustained pressure. Your skill tree includes movement skills, pet nodes, trap mechanics, and both melee and ranged damage options — giving you unusual flexibility at the cost of requiring more skill to play optimally.

## Ascendancy Paths

At level 50 you choose one of three paths — this choice is **permanent**:

| Ascendancy | Summary |
|---|---|
| [Shadowstalker](../ascendancies/shadowstalker.md) | Melee burst assassin — invisibility, poison, critical strikes |
| [Beastmaster](../ascendancies/beastmaster.md) | Pet army commander — animal companions and mythological summons |
| [Pathfinder](../ascendancies/pathfinder.md) | Tactical survivalist — tanky kiting, traps, self-sufficiency |

## Skill Tree Highlights

[TODO: Add skill tree node highlights once tree is designed]

## Tips

- Archers are the only class with access to both melee and ranged damage nodes in the base tree.
- Shadowstalker requires excellent positioning — stealth entry + burst + escape is the full loop.
- Beastmaster scales with the number and quality of active pets; invest in pet stat nodes early.
```

## Task 5: Warrior Ascendancies (Berserker, Guardian, Paladin)

**Files:**
- Create: `docs/ascendancies/berserker.md`
- Create: `docs/ascendancies/guardian.md`
- Create: `docs/ascendancies/paladin.md`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create `docs/ascendancies/berserker.md`**

```markdown
---
tags: [ascendancy, warrior, berserker, damage, lifesteal]
---

# Berserker

**Class:** [Warrior](../classes/warrior.md) · **Role:** Melee DPS

> *Pain is fuel. Rage is armor. Death is just another resource.*

The Berserker abandons all pretense of defense and transforms injury into power. Every wound deepens the rage, every point of missing health multiplies the carnage. Berserkers are glass cannons that refuse to break — because they keep stealing life back from everything they hit.

## Core Mechanics

| Mechanic | How It Works |
|---|---|
| **Rage Stacking** | Rage builds passively in combat and is consumed by empowered attacks. Higher Rage = higher damage multiplier. |
| **Low HP Scaling** | Ascendancy nodes give flat and percentage damage bonuses that increase as your current HP drops below thresholds (75%, 50%, 25%). |
| **Lifesteal** | A percentage of physical damage dealt is returned as health. Keeps you alive at low HP, enabling the low-HP bonuses. |

## Playstyle

Berserker is a high-risk, high-reward melee damage dealer. You deliberately operate at low health to maximize damage multipliers, relying on lifesteal and burst windows to stay alive. The gameplay loop is: **build Rage → enter low-HP threshold → lifesteal through the burst window → repeat**.

## Ascendancy Tree Focus

Ascendancy tree nodes for Berserker emphasize:

- Increasing Rage generation rate and max stack cap
- Adding more low-HP damage thresholds and stronger multipliers per threshold
- Improving lifesteal rate and adding on-kill healing bursts
- Reducing skill cooldowns while Rage is above a set value

[TODO: Add full ascendancy tree node list once designed]

## Recommended Skill Slot Setup

[TODO: Add recommended keybind layout once skill list is finalized]

## Synergies

- Gear with **lifesteal %** and **attack speed** amplifies the burst window enormously.
- Movement skills assigned to keybinds let you dash into melee range before Rage decays.
- Avoid stacking too much health — it pushes you away from the low-HP thresholds.
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Create `docs/ascendancies/guardian.md`**

```markdown
---
tags: [ascendancy, warrior, guardian, tank, support]
---

# Guardian

**Class:** [Warrior](../classes/warrior.md) · **Role:** Tank / Aggro Management

> *The line holds because I hold it.*

The Guardian is the definitive tank of Ashen Pantheon. Where the Berserker bleeds and the Paladin heals, the Guardian does the unglamorous but essential work: drawing every threat, absorbing every hit, and making sure their party never has to.

## Core Mechanics

| Mechanic | How It Works |
|---|---|
| **Taunt** | Forces nearby enemies and bosses to redirect their attacks to the Guardian for a duration. Essential for party protection. |
| **Party Shields** | Grants temporary absorption shields to party members. Shields absorb a flat amount of damage before health is affected. |
| **Aggro Management** | Passive nodes ensure the Guardian generates more threat than any other party member, keeping enemies focused on them. |

## Playstyle

Guardian is the only ascendancy built around protecting others. In solo play, you are nearly unkillable but kill slowly. In a party, you are the backbone — your Taunt ability lets allies operate freely, your Party Shields buy crucial seconds, and your aggro management ensures the Mage standing in the back never gets touched.

## Ascendancy Tree Focus

Ascendancy tree nodes for Guardian emphasize:

- Increasing Taunt duration and the number of enemies affected
- Strengthening Party Shield absorption values and cooldown reduction
- Passive threat generation multipliers
- Damage reduction while taunting
- Counter-damage — reflecting a portion of absorbed hits back at attackers

[TODO: Add full ascendancy tree node list once designed]

## Recommended Skill Slot Setup

[TODO: Add recommended keybind layout once skill list is finalized]

## Synergies

- Guardian is most powerful in a 4-player party with at least one healer (Paladin).
- Gear focused on **physical defense**, **health pool**, and **cooldown reduction** maximizes uptime on Taunt.
- In solo play, pair Guardian's defense with the highest damage skills available to compensate for slow clear speed.
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Create `docs/ascendancies/paladin.md`**

```markdown
---
tags: [ascendancy, warrior, paladin, holy, healer, support]
---

# Paladin

**Class:** [Warrior](../classes/warrior.md) · **Role:** Holy DPS / Party Support

> *Divine fire cleanses what steel cannot.*

The Paladin walks the line between warrior and priest. They deal devastating area-of-effect holy damage, mend wounds with blessed light, and wrap their party in powerful auras that make everyone fight better. The Paladin is the only Warrior ascendancy that meaningfully supports a party, making them one of the most sought-after players in group content.

## Core Mechanics

| Mechanic | How It Works |
|---|---|
| **AoE Holy Damage** | Paladin skills release bursts of holy energy in areas around the caster, dealing magic damage based on the character's holy power stat. |
| **Heals** | Targeted and AoE heals restore health to self and party members. Scales with holy power. |
| **Party Aura Buffs** | Persistent auras that radiate from the Paladin and affect all party members within range — boosting damage, defense, or movement speed. |

## Playstyle

Paladin rewards players who can juggle offensive and supportive responsibilities simultaneously. In boss fights, you are managing your aura selection, throwing out heals when party members take spike damage, and weaving in AoE holy bursts on the boss. You are never standing still.

## Ascendancy Tree Focus

Ascendancy tree nodes for Paladin emphasize:

- Expanding AoE radius and damage of holy burst skills
- Increasing heal potency and adding over-time heal effects
- Unlocking additional aura types (offensive, defensive, utility)
- Reducing aura swap cooldown so you can react to changing fight phases
- Adding holy damage bonuses that scale with current mana

[TODO: Add full ascendancy tree node list once designed]

## Recommended Skill Slot Setup

[TODO: Add recommended keybind layout once skill list is finalized]

## Synergies

- Paladin auras stack with all party member buffs — coordinate with your Berserker for massive damage windows.
- Gear should prioritize **holy power**, **mana pool**, and **cooldown reduction**.
- In solo play, lean into the AoE holy damage build — clear speed compensates for the lack of party synergy.
```

## Task 6: Mage Ascendancies (Elementalist, Arcanist, Hexblade)

**Files:**

- [ ] **Step 1: Create `docs/ascendancies/elementalist.md`**

```markdown
---
tags: [ascendancy, mage, elementalist, fire, ice, lightning, aoe]
---

# Elementalist

**Class:** [Mage](../classes/mage.md) · **Role:** Elemental AoE DPS

> *Why choose one element when you can use all three at once?*

The Elementalist is the apex predator of area-of-effect damage. They do not specialize in a single element — they weaponize the reactions between Fire, Ice, and Lightning to create cascading damage chains that can devastate entire boss phases in seconds.

## Core Mechanics

| Mechanic | How It Works |
|---|---|
| **Elemental Mixing** | Applying two different elements to the same target creates an elemental reaction (e.g., Fire + Ice = Shatter). Each reaction deals bonus damage and applies a status effect. |
| **AoE Bursts** | Elementalist signature skills release large area bursts of a chosen element, letting you apply elements to entire groups simultaneously. |
| **Elemental Mastery** | Passive stat that increases the damage and effect of all elemental reactions triggered. |

## Elemental Reactions

| Element A | Element B | Reaction | Effect |
|---|---|---|---|
| Fire | Ice | Shatter | Massive burst damage + freeze |
| Fire | Lightning | Overload | Chain lightning to nearby enemies |
| Ice | Lightning | Superconductor | AoE slow + defense reduction |

[TODO: Finalize reaction table once mechanics are locked]

## Ascendancy Tree Focus

- Increasing Elemental Mastery stat and reaction damage multipliers
- Reducing elemental application cooldowns
- Unlocking passive elemental aura (constantly applying a chosen element to nearby enemies)
- Adding fourth-element nodes for rare reactions

[TODO: Add full ascendancy tree node list once designed]

## Recommended Skill Slot Setup

[TODO: Add recommended keybind layout once skill list is finalized]

## Synergies

- Stack **Elemental Mastery** on all gear pieces — it multiplies reaction damage.
- Assign one element per skill slot and rotate in order to maximize reaction uptime.
- Elementalist pairs exceptionally well with Arcanist support in parties.
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Create `docs/ascendancies/arcanist.md`**

```markdown
---
tags: [ascendancy, mage, arcanist, summoner, pets, support]
---

# Arcanist

**Class:** [Mage](../classes/mage.md) · **Role:** Elemental Summoner / Support

> *Why fight when your creations can fight for you?*

The Arcanist forges living constructs from elemental energy and sends them into battle. Part summoner, part strategist, the Arcanist's power scales with how well you manage your elemental entities — positioning them, directing their abilities, and keeping them alive long enough to matter.

## Core Mechanics

| Mechanic | How It Works |
|---|---|
| **Elemental Summons** | Conjure persistent entities from Fire, Ice, or Lightning energy. Each type has unique behaviors and abilities. |
| **Pet Micromanagement** | Arcanist pets can be directed to specific targets or positions. Active management dramatically increases their effectiveness. |
| **Support Aura** | Arcanist passively emits an aura that buffs nearby allied pets and party members based on active summon types. |

## Summon Types

| Summon | Element | Behavior |
|---|---|---|
| Flame Wraith | Fire | Aggressive melee attacker, applies burn on hit |
| Frost Construct | Ice | Defensive, taunts enemies away from Arcanist |
| Storm Wisp | Lightning | Ranged, chains lightning between targets |

[TODO: Expand summon roster as designed]

## Ascendancy Tree Focus

- Increasing max active summon count
- Boosting individual summon stats (damage, health, speed)
- Unlocking hybrid-element summons
- Reducing summon cast cost and cooldown
- Buffing the support aura radius and effect

[TODO: Add full ascendancy tree node list once designed]

## Recommended Skill Slot Setup

[TODO: Add recommended keybind layout once skill list is finalized]

## Synergies

- Pairs powerfully with Elementalist in a party — Arcanist summons apply elements continuously, setting up reactions for the Elementalist.
- Gear should focus on **summon damage**, **max mana**, and **cooldown reduction**.
- In solo play, build tanky summons to absorb boss attention while you cast from safety.
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Create `docs/ascendancies/hexblade.md`**

```markdown
---
tags: [ascendancy, mage, hexblade, curses, dots, auras, chaos]
---

# Hexblade

**Class:** [Mage](../classes/mage.md) · **Role:** Chaos DoT / Debuffer

> *The curse lands. Then the rot begins. Then you wait.*

The Hexblade is a patient predator. They do not burst enemies down in a flash of lightning — they hex, curse, and corrode them. Hexblades excel at multi-target scenarios, applying devastating damage-over-time effects and auras that make everything weaker for themselves and their party.

## Core Mechanics

| Mechanic | How It Works |
|---|---|
| **AoE Auras** | Persistent fields centered on the Hexblade that apply ongoing effects to enemies within range. Multiple auras can stack. |
| **Curses** | Applied directly to targets. Curses amplify all damage taken by a percentage and can unlock bonus effects for other skills. |
| **Chaos DoT** | Damage-over-time effects that deal chaos (untyped) damage, bypassing elemental resistances entirely. |

## Playstyle

Hexblade is a slow-burn damage dealer. Your opener is always: apply Curse → drop AoE auras → stack DoTs. Once set up, you deal massive sustained damage with minimal input. Against bosses with multiple phases, you must re-apply effects after each phase transition.

## Ascendancy Tree Focus

- Increasing Curse potency and the number of simultaneous Curses per target
- Extending AoE aura radius and adding new aura types
- Boosting Chaos DoT tick rate and damage
- Adding Curse synergy nodes (e.g., cursed targets take bonus lightning damage)
- Unlocking a Hexblade passive that makes a percentage of all damage dealt ignore resistances

[TODO: Add full ascendancy tree node list once designed]

## Recommended Skill Slot Setup

[TODO: Add recommended keybind layout once skill list is finalized]

## Synergies

- Curses benefit every party member — Hexblade is a strong support pick in 4-player groups.
- Gear should prioritize **DoT damage**, **curse duration**, and **aura effectiveness**.
- Avoid building attack speed — Hexblade's damage comes from setup, not spam.
```

## Task 7: Archer Ascendancies (Shadowstalker, Beastmaster, Pathfinder)

```markdown
---
tags: [ascendancy, archer, shadowstalker, stealth, poison, crits]
---

# Shadowstalker

**Class:** [Archer](../classes/archer.md) · **Role:** Melee Burst Assassin

> *You heard nothing. You saw nothing. You just lost 80% of your health.*

The Shadowstalker is the only Archer ascendancy that fights in melee. They use ranged tools as openers and escape mechanisms, then close in for devastating burst windows amplified by poison and critical hits. Mastering the Shadowstalker means mastering the approach — every engagement should begin and end on your terms.

## Core Mechanics

| Mechanic | How It Works |
|---|---|
| **Invisibility** | Enter a stealth state that makes you untargetable for a duration or until you attack. Breaking stealth with an attack grants a crit multiplier bonus. |
| **Poison** | A stacking damage-over-time applied by specific skills. Each stack deals independent damage; high stacks deal enormous sustained damage. |
| **Critical Strikes** | Shadowstalker ascendancy nodes dramatically increase critical hit chance and multiplier, especially on the first hit after stealth. |

## Playstyle

Engage from stealth → heavy critical opener → apply poison stacks → retreat to range → re-enter stealth → repeat. The loop rewards players who can manage distance and cooldowns precisely.

## Ascendancy Tree Focus

- Increasing stealth duration and reducing reveal cooldown
- Adding bonuses to the "first hit from stealth" window
- Scaling poison stacks (more stacks, higher individual stack damage)
- Boosting critical hit chance and multiplier passively
- Unlocking a node that refunds stealth cooldown on kill

[TODO: Add full ascendancy tree node list once designed]

## Recommended Skill Slot Setup

[TODO: Add recommended keybind layout once skill list is finalized]

## Synergies

- Stack **critical hit chance** and **poison damage** on gear.
- Movement skills are critical — assign at least two to always have an escape route.
- Hexblade curses further amplify Shadowstalker poison — a powerful party combo.
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Create `docs/ascendancies/beastmaster.md`**

```markdown
# Beastmaster

**Class:** [Archer](../classes/archer.md) · **Role:** Pet Army / Summoner

> *Every myth ever told about a monster? I've tamed one.*

The Beastmaster commands a growing menagerie of animal companions and — uniquely — mythological creatures drawn from the same pantheons as the game's bosses. Where the Arcanist creates elemental constructs, the Beastmaster bonds with living things. Your pets level with you, learn abilities, and can even respond to vocal commands mid-combat.

## Core Mechanics

| Mechanic | How It Works |
|---|---|
| **Animal Companions** | Natural animals (wolves, eagles, bears) that fight alongside you. Each has unique abilities and a basic AI that can be overridden with commands. |
| **Mythological Companions** | Rarer, more powerful creatures based on mythological beasts — unlocked through specific in-game events. |
| **Companion Synergies** | Certain pet combinations unlock bonus effects (e.g., wolf + eagle = coordinated flanking bonus). |

## Pet Roster (Starter)

| Pet | Type | Role |
|---|---|---|
| Iron Wolf | Animal | Melee attacker, pack bonus with multiple wolves |
| Sky Eagle | Animal | Ranged, scouts unseen areas, dives on targets |
| Stone Bear | Animal | Tank, draws enemy attention |

[TODO: Add mythological companion roster as designed]

## Ascendancy Tree Focus

- Increasing max active pet count
- Scaling pet stats (damage, health, ability power)
- Unlocking mythological companion slots
- Adding companion synergy nodes
- Giving the Beastmaster a passive aura that buffs all active pets

[TODO: Add full ascendancy tree node list once designed]

## Recommended Skill Slot Setup

[TODO: Add recommended keybind layout once skill list is finalized]

## Synergies

- Gear that boosts **pet damage**, **pet health**, and **cooldown reduction** maximizes impact.
- In a party, Beastmaster pets can act as secondary tanks, letting Guardian focus on the boss.
- Pairs well with Arcanist — combined pet armies create overwhelming pressure.
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Create `docs/ascendancies/pathfinder.md`**

```markdown
---
tags: [ascendancy, archer, pathfinder, traps, survival, kiting]
---

# Pathfinder

**Class:** [Archer](../classes/archer.md) · **Role:** Tactical Survivalist / Trap Master

> *I was here before the fight started. I'll be here after it ends.*

The Pathfinder is the most self-sufficient character in Ashen Pantheon. They are the tankiest archer build, capable of kiting bosses indefinitely while a network of traps does continuous work. Pathfinders win by attrition — they outlast everything.

## Core Mechanics

| Mechanic | How It Works |
|---|---|
| **Traps** | Placed objects that trigger on enemy proximity, dealing damage, applying debuffs, or creating terrain effects. Multiple trap types can be active simultaneously. |
| **Kiting** | Pathfinder movement skills are designed for sustained repositioning — keeping enemies at range while maintaining damage output. |
| **Survival** | Pathfinder ascendancy nodes add significant health pool bonuses, damage reduction, and self-healing — making them far tankier than a standard Archer. |

## Trap Types

| Trap | Effect |
|---|---|
| Snare Trap | Roots enemy in place for 2 seconds |
| Explosive Trap | Burst physical damage in an area |
| Venom Trap | Applies poison stacks on trigger |
| Frost Trap | Slows enemy movement speed by 60% |

[TODO: Expand trap list once mechanics are finalized]

## Ascendancy Tree Focus

- Increasing max simultaneous trap count
- Boosting trap damage and trigger radius
- Adding passive trap re-arm mechanics (traps reset after a cooldown)
- Increasing personal health pool and passive damage reduction
- Unlocking a "prepared ground" passive that buffs the Pathfinder's damage while standing in a trap zone

[TODO: Add full ascendancy tree node list once designed]

## Recommended Skill Slot Setup

[TODO: Add recommended keybind layout once skill list is finalized]

## Synergies

- Stack **trap damage**, **movement speed**, and **health** on gear.
- Pathfinder is the best solo class for learning boss patterns — you can survive long enough to observe every phase.
- In a party, Pathfinder traps provide reliable AoE damage without requiring attention, freeing mental load for mechanics.
```

## Task 8: Systems Section

**Files:**

- [ ] **Step 1: Create `docs/systems/progression.md`**

```markdown
---
tags: [systems, progression, leveling]
---

# Progression

Ashen Pantheon uses a **two-phase progression system** divided at level 50. Each phase has its own skill tree, its own pacing, and its own focus. Reaching level 100 opens endgame content.

## The Full Arc

| Phase | Levels | Focus |
|---|---|---|
| **Foundation** | 1 – 49 | Main skill tree, class skills, broad build construction |
| **Ascendancy Choice** | 50 | One permanent ascendancy selected — no going back |
| **Mastery** | 51 – 100 | Ascendancy skill tree, specialization deepens |
| **Endgame** | 100 | Gear farming, challenge bosses, rankings |

## Phase 1: Foundation (Levels 1–49)

You earn **1 skill point per level**, giving you 49 points to spend in the main skill tree by the time you hit the ascendancy gate.

The main skill tree is broad:
- Shared nodes available to all classes
- Class-specific nodes exclusive to your base class
- Many nodes — you will not fill the tree in 49 points; choices matter
- Node types: passive stat boosts, active skill unlocks, and modifier nodes that alter existing skills

See [Skill Tree](skill-tree.md) for full details.

## The Ascendancy Gate (Level 50)

At level 50 your progression pauses until you complete the **Ascendancy Trial** — a solo instance designed to test the playstyle of your chosen ascendancy. On completion, you permanently lock in one of your class's three ascendancy paths.

**This choice cannot be undone.** Choose carefully.

## Phase 2: Mastery (Levels 51–100)

After choosing your ascendancy you enter the second phase. You earn **1 ascendancy point per 2 levels** (levels 52, 54, 56... up to 100), giving you **25 total ascendancy points**.

The ascendancy tree is focused:
- Far fewer nodes than the main tree
- Each node is powerful and identity-defining
- Nodes build on each other — later nodes assume earlier ones are active

See [Ascendancy Tree](ascendancy-tree.md) for full details.

## Endgame (Level 100)

At level 100 the progression systems close and the endgame opens:

- **Gear farming** — hunting specific boss drops to maximize your build
- **Challenge bosses** — harder versions of existing bosses with new mechanics
- **Rankings** — compete for clear speed records and leaderboard positions

[TODO: Expand endgame content section once designed]
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Create `docs/systems/skill-tree.md`**

```markdown
---
tags: [systems, skill-tree, leveling]
---

# Skill Tree (Levels 1–50)

The main skill tree is the foundation of every build in Ashen Pantheon. It is a large, branching network of nodes that you navigate from level 1 through level 49, spending one point per level.

## Overview

- **49 points** available (one per level, levels 1–49)
- Tree is **shared across classes** at the outer edges, with **class-exclusive sections** in the center
- Node types: passive stats, active skill unlocks, and skill modifiers
- You cannot fill the entire tree — builds are defined by what you choose to skip

## Node Types

| Type | Description |
|---|---|
| **Passive Stat Node** | Directly increases a stat (e.g., +5% physical damage, +20 max health) |
| **Skill Unlock Node** | Adds a new active skill to your available skill pool |
| **Modifier Node** | Changes the behavior of a specific skill (e.g., "Fire Ball now hits twice but costs 20% more mana") |
| **Keystone Node** | Rare, powerful nodes that fundamentally change how a mechanic works. Located deep in the tree. |

## Class Sections

Each class has an exclusive section of the tree that only they can access:

| Class | Tree Section Focus |
|---|---|
| Warrior | Melee damage, armor scaling, stance nodes |
| Mage | Elemental affinity, cast speed, mana efficiency |
| Archer | Range multipliers, mobility skills, pet slot nodes |

## Build Philosophy

The skill tree rewards specialization. Spreading points evenly produces a weak generalist build. Strong builds commit to a clear path:

1. Rush toward key Skill Unlock nodes early
2. Fill in passive stat nodes along the path
3. Pick up Modifier nodes for your core active skills
4. Consider Keystone nodes as long-term goals

[TODO: Add visual tree diagram once designed]
[TODO: Add full node list once tree is finalized]
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Create `docs/systems/ascendancy-tree.md`**

```markdown
---
tags: [systems, ascendancy-tree, endgame]
---

# Ascendancy Tree (Levels 50–100)

The ascendancy tree is the second phase of character progression, unlocked after you choose your ascendancy at level 50. It is a smaller, more powerful tree exclusive to your chosen ascendancy path.

## Overview

- **25 points** available (one per 2 levels from 52 to 100)
- Tree is **exclusive to your ascendancy** — Berserker and Guardian trees are completely different
- Nodes are fewer but far more impactful than main skill tree nodes
- Tree is designed to amplify and complete the build direction started in the main tree

## Pacing

| Level Range | Points Earned | Cumulative Total |
|---|---|---|
| 50 | 0 (tree unlocked, no points yet) | 0 |
| 52 | 1 | 1 |
| 54 | 1 | 2 |
| ... | ... | ... |
| 100 | 1 | 25 |

## Node Power Level

Ascendancy nodes are intentionally strong. Examples of the tier of power:

- "Your critical strikes deal 40% more damage"
- "Lifesteal heals for double the amount when below 50% health"
- "Taunt now pulls all enemies in the instance to your location"

These are build-defining, not incremental.

## Synergy with Main Tree

The ascendancy tree assumes you have built the main tree to support it. For example, Berserker ascendancy nodes that scale with Rage assume you have unlocked Rage-generating skills in the main tree.

Plan your main tree with your intended ascendancy in mind from character creation.

[TODO: Add individual ascendancy tree diagrams once designed]
[TODO: Add full node lists per ascendancy]
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Create `docs/systems/skill-slots.md`**

```markdown
---
tags: [systems, skill-slots, keybinds]
---

# Skill Slots

Ashen Pantheon uses a **free keybind system** — any skill you have unlocked can be assigned to any keybind slot, with no restrictions.

## How It Works

1. **Unlock skills** via the skill tree (Skill Unlock nodes) and ascendancy tree
2. Open the **Skills panel** to see all unlocked skills
3. **Drag any skill** to any empty keybind slot
4. Your keybind is now active in combat

## No Restrictions

There is no concept of a "skill type" that limits assignment. You can:

- Fill every slot with damage skills
- Mix damage, defense, and movement skills freely
- Assign healing skills alongside crowd-control skills
- Run zero defensive skills if you prefer maximum aggression (not recommended)

This means the optimal skill layout is a meaningful build decision, not a default.

## Keybind Slots

[TODO: Add exact number of keybind slots once finalized]

Standard layout includes slots for:
- Primary attack
- Secondary attack
- Utility / movement
- Active defensive
- Special / signature skill

All slots are freely reassignable. The suggested labels above are for reference only.

## Build Implications

The free keybind system interacts with build design in important ways:

- Skill modifier nodes in the skill tree only apply when the modified skill is slotted
- Some ascendancy nodes grant passive bonuses to "any movement skill slotted"
- You can create highly specialized setups (all damage) or flexible setups (mixed toolkit)

[TODO: Add example keybind layouts per ascendancy once skills are finalized]
```

- [ ] **Step 5: Create `docs/systems/instancing.md`**

```markdown
---
tags: [systems, instancing, multiplayer]
---

# Instancing

Ashen Pantheon uses a **player-owned instancing system**. Every player controls their own copy of the game world, with the option to invite others.

## Your World Instance

When you log in, you enter your personal instance — a version of the game world that belongs exclusively to you. You control:

- **Progression state** — which bosses you have killed, which zones are cleared
- **Access** — who can enter your world
- **Pace** — there is no server-wide day cycle or shared resource depletion

Nobody can enter your instance without your invitation.

## Party System

You can invite up to **3 other players** to your instance, creating a party of 4.

| Action | How |
|---|---|
| Invite a player | Search by name in the Social panel → Send Instance Invite |
| Accept an invite | Notification appears → Accept to join their instance |
| Leave a party | Party panel → Leave Instance (you return to your own world) |
| Kick a party member | Party leader only — Party panel → Kick Player |

When a party member enters your instance, their progression in **your** world does not affect their own instance. Boss kills in your instance count for you; you must enter their instance for them to get credit.

## Global Layer

Even inside your personal instance, a **global layer** connects you to all players:

| Feature | Description |
|---|---|
| **Global Chat** | Persistent text channels shared by all players regardless of instance |
| **Rankings** | Live leaderboards for level, boss kills, and gear score — updated in real time |
| **Trading** | List items for sale and purchase items from other players across instances |

The global layer ensures the game feels like a living world even within solo instancing.

## Boss Instance Scaling

Boss encounters scale based on party size:

| Party Size | Boss HP Multiplier | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | 1× | Solo tuning — designed to be clearable |
| 2 | 1.6× | Slight increase |
| 3 | 2.1× | Moderate increase |
| 4 | 2.5× | Full party tuning |

[TODO: Add exact scaling values once tuned in playtesting]
```

- [ ] **Step 1: Create `docs/bosses/index.md`**

```markdown
---
tags: [bosses, overview]
---

# Bosses

Bosses are the heart of Ashen Pantheon. Every major boss is drawn from a real-world mythology and designed to be a memorable, escalating encounter — not a damage sponge with a health bar.

## Design Philosophy

Each boss is built around three principles:

1. **Mythological authenticity** — every boss is rooted in real mythology. Their visual design, abilities, and lore reflect their actual mythological origin.
2. **Multi-phase fights** — bosses evolve as the fight progresses. A boss at 100% health fights differently than the same boss at 20% health.
3. **Learn-able patterns** — bosses are hard but fair. Every attack has a tell. Every mechanic can be understood and countered.

## Mythologies

Ashen Pantheon draws from five real-world mythological traditions:

| Mythology | Region | Themes |
|---|---|---|
| **Greek** | Mediterranean | Hubris, fate, divine punishment |
| **Norse** | Scandinavia | Doom, honor, the end of all things |
| **Egyptian** | North Africa | Judgment, the afterlife, cosmic order |
| **Japanese** | East Asia | Purity, spirits, the boundary between worlds |
| **Slavic** | Eastern Europe | Nature, dark forests, old gods |

## Boss Roster

[TODO: Add individual boss pages and link them here as bosses are designed and named]

Each boss will have a dedicated page covering:
- Lore and mythological background
- Phase breakdown
- Attack patterns and counters
- Recommended builds and party compositions
- Drop table
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Create `docs/bosses/boss-phases.md`**

```markdown
---
tags: [bosses, mechanics, phases]
---

# Boss Phases

All major bosses in Ashen Pantheon use a **multi-phase fight system**. As a boss's health drops, the encounter changes — new mechanics appear, existing ones become more dangerous, and the visual presentation escalates.

## Why Phases Matter

A single-phase boss fight is a test of one skill set. A multi-phase boss fight is a test of adaptability. Ashen Pantheon bosses are designed so that the strategy that works at 100% health does not simply work at 20% health — you must adjust.

## Standard Phase Structure

Most bosses use a three-phase structure, though this varies:

| Phase | HP Threshold | Typical Changes |
|---|---|---|
| Phase 1 | 100% – 66% | Core moveset, learning phase, moderate difficulty |
| Phase 2 | 66% – 33% | New abilities added, arena may change, damage increases |
| Phase 3 | 33% – 0% | Enraged state, highest damage, signature ultimate ability |

## Phase Transitions

Phase transitions are dramatic, scripted moments:

- Boss becomes temporarily invulnerable
- A cutscene or animation plays showing the boss's escalation
- The arena may change (new hazards appear, geometry shifts)
- The boss resets any stacks or statuses

Phase transitions are the safest moment to use consumables and communicate with your party.

## Enrage Timers

Some bosses have a soft enrage — if you do not reach a phase threshold within a time limit, the boss gains a permanent damage buff. This prevents excessively slow fights.

[TODO: Add enrage timer details per boss once tuned]

## Phase-Specific Mechanics

Many bosses have mechanics unique to a single phase:

- A Greek boss might summon divine champions only in Phase 2
- A Norse boss might trigger Ragnarok effects in Phase 3, filling the arena with rising water
- An Egyptian boss might require you to solve a puzzle during the Phase 1→2 transition

[TODO: Document per-boss phase mechanics as bosses are designed]
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Create `docs/world/index.md`**

```markdown
---
tags: [world, overview]
---

# World

The world of Ashen Pantheon is a realm caught between mythological pantheons. Millennia of divine warfare have shattered the natural order — zones that were once distinct regions have collapsed into each other, leaving a fractured landscape where Greek ruins stand beside Norse forests and Egyptian deserts bleed into Slavic marshes.

## The Ashen Realm

The world is called the **Ashen Realm** — named for the divine ash that rains from a sky that has not seen true sunlight since the gods began their war. The ash is not merely atmospheric; it is residual divine energy that empowers those who absorb enough of it. That is what you are: a mortal who has absorbed enough to fight back.

## World Structure

The world is divided into **zones** — distinct geographic regions, each themed around one of the game's mythological traditions. Zones contain:

- Exploration areas with enemies and resources
- Hidden locations with lore items
- One or more **boss arenas** — specially designed encounters that trigger when entered

## Instancing

The world is **player-instanced** — you explore your own copy of the Ashen Realm. See [Instancing](../systems/instancing.md) for full details.

## Global vs. Instance

| Layer | Who sees it | What it contains |
|---|---|---|
| Instance Layer | You (and your party) | Enemies, resources, boss states, your progression |
| Global Layer | All players | Chat, rankings, trading |

[TODO: Add zone list with map preview images once zones are named and designed]
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Create `docs/world/maps.md`**

```markdown
---

# Maps & Zones

The Ashen Realm is divided into **zones** — distinct geographic regions that players explore and clear. Each zone has a distinct visual theme drawn from one of the game's mythological traditions.

## Zone Overview

[TODO: Add full zone list as zones are designed and named]

## Zone Structure

Each zone contains:

| Element | Description |
|---|---|
| **Exploration Area** | Open map space with enemy encounters and environmental storytelling |
| **Resource Nodes** | Gatherable materials for crafting and trading |
| **Hidden Areas** | Optional side paths with lore items and rare enemies |
| **Boss Arena** | A locked area that opens when triggered — contains the zone's boss |
| **Instance Checkpoint** | Saves your position in the instance so you re-enter in the same zone |

## Zone Progression

Zones are intended to be completed in a general order based on player level, though the instanced nature means you can attempt any zone at any time:

[TODO: Add recommended zone order once zones are designed]

## Mythological Zone Themes

| Theme | Visual Style | Example Hazards |
|---|---|---|
| Greek | White marble ruins, olive groves, Aegean coastline | Lightning storms, cursed ground |
| Norse | Dark pine forests, frozen mountains, longship wreckage | Blizzards, spectral wolves |
| Egyptian | Desert sands, pyramid interiors, river deltas | Sand storms, plague swarms |
| Japanese | Cherry blossom forests, temple complexes, misty mountains | Spirit fire, illusion fields |
| Slavic | Deep marshes, dark forests, wooden fortress ruins | Cursed mists, animated forest |

[TODO: Add individual zone pages with map images, enemy lists, and boss location guides]
```

```markdown
# Devlog

Follow the development of **Ashen Pantheon** from the first idea to release. These entries are written directly by the developer — unfiltered progress updates, design decisions, lessons learned, and honest looks at what is and isn't working.

## Entries

| # | Title | Date |
|---|---|---|
| [001](001-the-beginning.md) | The Beginning | 2026-04-17 |

[TODO: New entries added as development progresses]
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Create `docs/devlog/001-the-beginning.md`**

```markdown
---
tags: [devlog, announcement]
---

# #001 — The Beginning

*April 17, 2026*

---

Every project starts somewhere. This one started with a frustration.

I've played a lot of action RPGs. I love the genre deeply — the satisfying click of a build coming together, the moment a boss's pattern finally clicks, the way a well-designed skill tree can hold your attention for hundreds of hours. But I kept running into the same walls: instancing systems that felt like afterthoughts, boss fights that were just stat checks dressed up in flashy animations, and ascendancy systems that felt like the designers ran out of budget halfway through.

So I started designing the game I wanted to play.

## What Ashen Pantheon Is

At its core, it's a **top-down 2D action RPG** — responsive, fast, skill-expressive. The camera sits above the character. Combat is immediate. Every fight is something you actively participate in, not something you watch your character do.

The instancing is first-class. Your world is yours. That means no competition for resources, no one ruining your boss attempt, no waiting for a respawn. But it doesn't mean isolation — the global layer keeps you connected to everyone else: chat is shared, rankings are live, trading works across instances.

The bosses are mythological. Not loosely inspired — actually pulled from real pantheons. Greek, Norse, Egyptian, Japanese, Slavic. I've been doing a lot of reading.

## The Three Classes

Three base classes: **Warrior**, **Mage**, **Archer**. Clean archetypes, but with room to go strange. Each branches into three ascendancies at level 50 — nine total paths, each with a distinct mechanical identity. The Hexblade is a chaos-DoT mage who curses targets and watches them rot. The Shadowstalker is an archer who closes to melee range and backstabs. The Pathfinder is a tank archer who wins by outlasting everything.

## The Two-Phase Skill System

Levels 1–50: broad skill tree, many nodes, class-specific paths, deep customization. Levels 50–100: focused ascendancy tree, 25 powerful points, 1 per 2 levels. The inflection at 50 is the most important decision in the game.

## Why This Wiki

I'm building this wiki as a public development document — it will grow as the game grows. Pages marked **[TODO]** aren't placeholders forever; they're honest signals about where development hasn't reached yet. When a skill tree gets designed, the wiki gets updated. When a boss gets named, the wiki gets its page.

This is a living document.

---

*Next entry will cover early combat prototyping and the first pass at class differentiation.*
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

```bash
git add docs/devlog/
git commit -m "feat: add devlog section with first entry"
```

## Task 11: Final Build Verification + Git Setup + Go Live

- [ ] **Step 1: Initialize git (if not already done)**

```bash
cd "C:/Users/mastu/Desktop/Ashen Pantheon"
git init
git add .
git commit -m "feat: complete Ashen Pantheon wiki scaffold"
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Local build verification**

```bash
mkdocs build --strict 2>&1
```

Expected: `INFO - Documentation built in X.XX seconds` with no ERRORs. Warnings about git dates are acceptable (git history is shallow locally). If you see a broken link error, track down the offending file and fix the link.

- [ ] **Step 3: Local preview (optional but recommended)**

```bash
mkdocs serve
```

Open `http://127.0.0.1:8000` in a browser. Verify:
- Dark amber theme renders correctly
- Logo appears in top-left
- All nav tabs visible (Home, Game Overview, Classes, Ascendancies, Systems, Bosses, World, Devlog, Tags)
- Tags index page loads
- At least one class page and one ascendancy page render correctly

Press `Ctrl+C` to stop the server.

- [ ] **Step 4: Create GitHub repository**

Option A — GitHub CLI (recommended):
```bash
gh repo create katassyn/ashen-pantheon --public --description "Official wiki for Ashen Pantheon ARPG"
```

Option B — Manual:
Go to https://github.com/new → name: `ashen-pantheon` → Public → Create repository

- [ ] **Step 5: Push to GitHub**

```bash
git remote add origin https://github.com/katassyn/ashen-pantheon.git
git branch -M main
git push -u origin main
```

- [ ] **Step 6: Enable GitHub Pages**

1. Go to `https://github.com/katassyn/ashen-pantheon/settings/pages`
2. Under **Source**, select **Deploy from a branch**
3. Branch: `gh-pages` / folder: `/ (root)`
4. Click **Save**

Note: The `gh-pages` branch is created automatically by the GitHub Actions workflow on first push. If it hasn't run yet, wait ~2 minutes after the push then set this.

- [ ] **Step 7: Verify deployment**

1. Go to `https://github.com/katassyn/ashen-pantheon/actions`
2. Confirm the `Deploy Wiki to GitHub Pages` workflow completed with a green checkmark
3. Open `https://katassyn.github.io/ashen-pantheon/`
4. Wiki is live ✓
